{{Quote|The owner of the ''Ebon Hawk'' is always welcome in the Dreshdae Cantina.|Mika Dorin greets Revan|translated=1|<ref name="kotor" />}}
'''The Drunk Side''', also known as the '''Dreshdae Cantina''', was a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] located within the settlement of [[Dreshdae]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], in operation during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. The cantina's [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]], who also managed the establishment, was the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Mika Dorin]]; he had taken over from the bar's [[Previous owner of The Drunk Side|previous owner]], who had run afoul of [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] over the establishment's operations. Along with [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] and [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] crews who ferried [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] from the surface, many of the instructors from the nearby [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] would stop by the establishment. [[Yuthura Ban]] was the most common face present, often staying there when visiting the colony. While there, she gave final approval to [[Sith hopeful]]s who had been given [[Sith Medallion|medallions]], which signified their worthiness to attend the Academy. When the former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]], who had been mindwiped and [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] by the [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Jedi Council]], came to Korriban in [[3956 BBY]], he met with Ban at the cantina, gaining her sponsorship to attend the Academy. The cantina was also home to Mika's under-the-table business that sold "premium items," such as [[Verpine prototype shield|prototype]] [[Verpine/Legends|Verpine]] [[Personal energy shield/Legends|shields]] and [[Medpac/Legends|life support]] packs to special customers.

==Description==
[[File:Mikadorin.jpg|thumb|left|190px|[[Mika Dorin]], the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina's]] owner and [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]].]]
The [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] itself was a simple affair, constructed as an extension of the [[Dreshdae]] settlement building, and only sparsely-decorated. Consisting of a single rectangular room, its long, U-shaped bar jutted out from the far wall nearly the full length of the establishment, while seats and small tables lined the sides.<ref name="kotor" />

==History==
Built alongside the rest of the settlement, which served as the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] hub of [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] operations within [[Sith Worlds/Legends|Sith Space]], the cantina served [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] of many different [[Species/Legends|species]]. During the [[Jedi Civil War]], [[Sith hopeful|hopefuls]] from around [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] gathered there, hoping to demonstrate to any passing [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] that they were worthy to attend the [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]] that had been established by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan's]] [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. Many of these hopefuls were largely ignored, though some were subjected to violent ends by the Academy's student body. At the same time, many [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] crews, contracted with the shipping of [[Sith artifact/Legends|Sith artifacts]] off-world from the excavations within the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], where many ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] were buried, loitered at the cantina in between jobs. When the establishment's [[Previous owner of The Drunk Side|first owner]] nearly ruined the cantina financially, earning the ire of Czerka Corporation, the [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]], [[Mika Dorin]], took over the bar's operations. He also began a side business selling "premium" arms and equipment to owners of the [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|''Dynamic''-class freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]''.<ref name="kotor" />

{{GameLSmechanics|Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic}}
When the mind-wiped and [[Jedi training/Legends|retrained]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Revan, accompanied by his comrades aboard the ''Hawk'', arrived on Korriban in [[3956 BBY]], he visited the cantina, seeking clues to the location of that planet's [[Star Map]]. This ancient piece of [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] technology provided data that, combined with information from other Maps, provided the location of the [[Star Forge]], the hidden weapons factory that was the pinnacle of the [[Infinite Empire/Legends|Infinite Empire]] and the power behind the Sith war effort. While asking various patrons about the artifact, Revan found that his vessel was the subject of admiration among the [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] and crew that frequented the place. Included among them was the bartender Dorin, who, along with a pair of [[Human/Legends|Human]] pilots, treated Revan to a brief and admiring history of the ''Hawk'' and the "services" it had performed in the past. This included describing the ship's true purpose, [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]], and information about those who had owned the vessel previously. Some time later, after securing a [[Sith Medallion]] from a student he had killed in order to defend a woman that the Sith had been threatening, Revan returned to the cantina to present the icon to the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] instructor [[Yuthura Ban]]. Ban, who was apprenticed to the [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] [[Uthar Wynn]], was considered second-in-command at the Academy, and was intrigued by Revan's power in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. She voiced her suspicions that there was more to Revan than he claimed. Revan, however, managed to persuade her that he wished to join the Sith, as getting into the Academy was the only way to reach the Valley and the Map that he suspected was to be found there.<ref name="kotor" />

[[File:Yuthuraban1234.jpg|thumb|right|200px|[[Yuthura Ban]] frequently met with [[Sith hopeful]]s at the cantina.]]
After being accepted into the Academy, Revan and one of his companions, the [[Cathar/Legends|Cathar]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Juhani]], met a Sith archaeologist named [[Dak Vesser]] in the Valley of the Dark Lords. Not wanting to have anything to do with either of them or whatever had brought them to Korriban, Vesser took refuge in the cantina prior to departing the planet, but not before telling Revan about the tombs in the Valley and speaking with Juhani about their past as fellow Jedi trainees. When the Academy fell into chaos following the death of the headmaster, Wynn, and the redemption of Ban to the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] following Revan's [[Skirmish on Korriban|double-cross and betrayal]] within the [[tomb of Naga Sadow]], the cantina continued to do business. Eventually, Dorin, with the assistance of his partner [[Ziagrom]], learned of Revan's true identity as the former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], and offered him access to the premium items they sold, including [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonators]], [[Medpac/Legends|life support]] packs, [[Verpine prototype shield|prototype]] [[Personal energy shield/Legends|personal shields]], and advanced [[Armor/Legends|armor]] suits. Prior to setting out for [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]] and the Star Forge, Revan again returned to the cantina to partake in this continuation of the under-the-table business that the pair had traditionally carried out with prior owners of the ''Ebon Hawk''.<ref name="kotor" />
{{Endgame}}

By [[3951 BBY]], Korriban was largely abandoned. When [[Meetra Surik]] arrived on the ancient Sith world sometime that year, she found the Academy deserted and access to Dreshdae blocked, forcing [[Atton Rand]], serving as pilot, to land the ''Hawk'' in the Valley of the Dark Lords itself. Infighting among the Sith in the years following the Jedi Civil War had rendered the planet virtually uninhabited.<ref name="kotor2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref>

==Inhabitants==
The Drunk Side was frequented primarily by transient beings, mostly Humans, Twi'leks, or Rodians, which included spacers on layover, freighter crews awaiting a new shipment off-planet, those hoping to become students at the Sith Academy that had been established not far from Dreshdae, and Sith students themselves. The most prominent patron was easily Yuthura Ban, who, when not teaching at the Academy, would loiter in the cantina, waiting to pass final judgment on those who had acquired Sith medallions. [[Pazaak/Legends|Pazaak]] was also a popular pastime at the cantina; the Rodian [[Toll Apkar]] was often present and willing to play a few hands.<ref name="kotor" />

==Behind the scenes==
The cantina was first introduced in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. Though it is referred to as the "Dreshdae Cantina" in dialogue, reading the sign next to the entrance shows the name, which is a play on the phrase "The [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Dark Side]]."<ref name="kotor" />

The player, as Revan, has a number of options dealing with the cantina. However, most of them are unrelated to the player's chosen alignment. With the exception of meeting Yuthura Ban, all other interactions that relate to The Drunk Side are completely optional. However, they do point toward the ultimate quest that has to do with that planet, which is finding the Star Map hidden in the tomb of [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]. While interacting with Yuthura, the player can choose to lie about their intentions if playing the [[canon]]ical [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light side]] storyline or, if following the dark path, can express an honest desire to join the Sith. If the player has Juhani with them when entering the Valley of the Dark Lords and talks with Dak Vesser, he will flee back to The Drunk Side, intending to run away from the Sith after being discovered. The player has the option to follow him back to the cantina, and either let him go or attempt to kill him; if Juhani is in the party, however, she will prevent the latter choice from occurring.<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

In addition, the "premium items" quest is not always available, due to how the quest is spawned in the wake of the [[Skirmish aboard the Leviathan|escape from the ''Leviathan'']]. It is not certain what triggers the appearance of Ziagrom, who gives the player the option of buying the items from Dorin at the cantina.<ref>[http://www.gamebanshee.com/starwarskotor/locations/dreshdae.php This page] lists information on accessing the "premium items" quest.</ref>
